feuds: Heather Gay: Heather challenges Monica's motives for being Jen Shah's unpaid assistant, playing an audio clip in which Monica compares herself to Kim Kardashian and frames the assistant role as a 'stepping stone,' implying Monica was using Jen to get on the show rather than genuinely helping a friend
key_moments:
Admits she was Jen Shah's unpaid assistant — 'She needed help at the time with... honestly her life. Like I would go and get her groceries. I would make sure that she was on time to her freaking meetings.'
Heather Gay plays an audio recording of Monica saying 'F***ing Kim Kardashian was a f***ing assistant. And look at that bitch now,' directly undercutting Monica's 'I was just a friend helping a friend' narrative
Monica partially concedes the point — 'I can fully admit that the whole reason why I was an assistant and putting up with that s*** was as a stepping stone' — before pivoting to defend herself
Monica reveals she cold-emailed Bravo casting, writing 'My name is Monica Fowler and your show sucks and your ratings are sh** and it's going to get canceled because you don't have the right cast'
Drops the bombshell that she proactively reached out to federal investigators as an informant against Jen Shah — 'I said, I have information about your case. I think that I did the right thing, and I think that everyone here probably should have done the right thing too.'
talking_points:
Address the assistant-to-cast-member pipeline head-on — acknowledge the Kim K audio clip but reframe it as ambition, not betrayal
Distinguish between 'having an agenda' and simply being opportunistic in a situation that was already collapsing around her
Own the informant decision confidently — position it as a moral choice, and put the implicit challenge back to the other women about why they stayed silent
Clarify the timeline: she didn't know Jen was on the show when they first became friends, and the 'stepping stone' mindset developed later
Be prepared to defend the casting email — it's eccentric but it actually reinforces her 'I got here on my own terms' narrative if she controls the spin
confessional_draft: People want to call it an agenda — I call it surviving. I walked away from a friendship, from an unpaid job, and ultimately from my own silence when I knew what was right. If using every resource available to me to build my own life makes me calculating, then fine, but at least I'm not sitting across from a federal case pretending I didn't see it coming.
show: Real Housewives of Salt Lake City

feuds: Monica Garcia: Heather positions herself as the truth-teller of the reunion, ambushing Monica with a pre-prepared audio recording to expose what she sees as Monica's fake 'I was just a good friend' narrative and ulterior motives for working with Jen Shah
key_moments:
Produces and plays an audio clip of Monica's Kim Kardashian quote mid-interrogation — 'I would like you to hear something, Monica, that you said' — a clearly premeditated move designed to publicly discredit Monica's story
Delivers the flat accusation directly to Monica's face: 'I just was just clarifying that I don't think you were her friend helping her. I think that you had an agenda.'
Draws a distinction between herself and Monica on the question of applying for the show — 'I didn't apply, I helped bring the show to Utah' — subtly asserting her own legitimacy while undermining Monica's
talking_points:
Stand firmly behind playing the audio clip — this wasn't a gotcha, it was receipts, and receipts matter in this group
Clarify what the 'agenda' accusation means specifically: Monica misrepresented her relationship with Jen as pure friendship when it was transactional from the start
Be ready to address the 'everyone had to apply' comment — Lisa will likely push back on the 'I brought the show to Utah' distinction
Acknowledge Monica's informant revelation without letting it completely rehabilitate her — motive still matters even if the outcome was right
confessional_draft: I didn't come to that reunion stage without my receipts, honey. Monica wants to rewrite history and paint herself as this loyal friend who just happened to land on a reality TV show — but I had her own voice on tape telling a very different story. The truth doesn't need my help, it just needs a good speaker system.
show: Real Housewives of Salt Lake City

key_moments: Interjects to correct Monica's claim that everyone 'applied' to be on the show — 'I didn't, I didn't apply, I like help bring the show to Utah so I didn't apply' — asserting her founding-cast status
talking_points:
Maintain the distinction between original cast members who built the show and newer cast members who came through open casting
Be ready to weigh in on the Jen Shah informant revelation and what, if anything, the other women knew or should have done
Don't let Monica's chaos overshadow your own storylines from the season
confessional_draft: There's a difference between building something from the ground up and showing up after the foundation is already poured. I helped bring this show to life — and I'll be damned if that gets glossed over in a conversation about who had an agenda and who didn't.
show: Real Housewives of Salt Lake City
